applied_optimizations.name: Triton fused ReLU + bias kernel
applied_optimizations.description: Replaced separate PyTorch ReLU and bias add with a fused Triton kernel. Uses flat element indexing with BLOCK_SIZE=1024 for efficient memory coalescing. Each program handles 1024 elements, computing channel index for bias lookup from flat index.

attempted_but_failed.name: Full Triton conv2d kernel
attempted_but_failed.reason: Complexity and tensor size limits - Triton compilation failed with 'numel exceeds maximum' errors
attempted_but_failed.error: ValueError('numel (8388608) exceeds triton maximum tensor numel (1048576)')

attempted_but_failed.name: torch.compile
attempted_but_failed.reason: Compilation failure on AMD MI355X - MLIR pass pipeline failed

notes: Reference model: Conv2d(64->128, k=3) + ReLU + bias on 128x64x128x128 input
Baseline: 3.72ms (PyTorch Conv2d via MIOpen + separate ReLU + bias)
Best Triton approach: PyTorch Conv2d + fused Triton(ReLU+bias) at 3.36ms (1.10x speedup)
Full Triton conv2d not feasible - MIOpen convolution is highly optimized
The 0.36ms improvement comes from fusing ReLU+bias, saving one kernel launch and memory round-trip
Further optimization limited by MIOpen conv dominance (70% of runtime)
